title: Add and subtract within 1000 word problems
description: Practice one-step addition and subtraction word problems with numbers less than 1000.
topic_path: Module 2: Place value and problem solving with units of measure
Topic E: Two- and three-digit measurement subtraction using the standard algorithm
Add and subtract within 1000 word problems

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $461$ homes in the part of town where Samir lives. He counted that $352$ of those homes are apartments. 

**How many of the homes are not apartments?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] homes
hints:
There are $\purpleD{461}$ homes. Of those, $\maroonD{352}$ homes are apartments, and the rest are not apartments.

![A tape diagram models that the 461 homes are equal to the 352 apartments and the unknown number that are not apartments.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/edb8f88af7332fe6e62d8c4c7d2eb1f4dc12b141)

$\purpleD{461}-\maroonD{352}=\blueE{?}$

![Place value blocks show 461 blocks with 352 blocks crossed out](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/00a4f08d27e4bd8e6a405a757f7c0d9570a59b79)
There are $\blueE{109}$ homes that are not apartments.

assessment_items.question_content:
Noel had $300$ index cards in a pack. She used $183$ cards.

**How many cards are left?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cards
hints:
There were $\purpleD{300}$ cards. Of those, Noel has used $\maroonD{183}$ cards, and the rest are left.

![A tape diagram models that the 300 cards are equal to the 183 used cards and the unknown number that are left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a034dfceace88198509db4c13281986d427a7c1c)

$\purpleD{300}-\maroonD{183}=\blueE{?}$

![Place value blocks show 300 blocks with 183 blocks crossed out](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fab9bbfd15bb4bdeb82b673900933ebd23d62058)
There are $\blueE{117}$ cards left.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ling has read $118$ pages of a book. The book has $141$ pages.

**How many pages are left?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] pages
hints:
The book has $\purpleD{141}$ pages. Of those, Ling has read $\maroonD{118}$ pages, and the rest are left.

![A tape diagram models that the 141 total pages are equal to the 118 read pages and the unknown number that are left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8fd44e65786b11e3fa6edebdc1e664dd1faffd7d)

$\purpleD{141}-\maroonD{118}=\blueE{?}$

![Place value blocks show 141 blocks with 118 blocks crossed out](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c8a9c66f6728659a0326482818a0e3308058fd94)
There are $\blueE{23}$ pages left.

assessment_items.question_content:
Milan has collected $363$ shells so far. He wants to collect $581$ shells in all. 

**How many more shells does Milan want?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] shells
hints:
Milan wants to collect $\purpleD{581}$ shells. He has already collected $\maroonD{363}$ shells, and he has not collected the rest yet.

![A tape diagram models that the 581 shells are equal to the 363 shells collected and the unknown number that are not collected yet.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a84ba02a18dfbacfe80f6bee0ea1b300afc5b6ba)

$\purpleD{581}-\maroonD{363}=\blueE{?}$

![Part 1 of 2. Place value blocks show 581 blocks with 363 blocks crossed out](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c5d43b697030955f9315b50a2ae1a564e0a22607)

![Part 2 of 2 of previous image.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c73e30a631949278caf9b3bebab093243e7fe487)
Milan wants to collect $\blueE{218}$ more shells.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table gives the numbers of kinds of insects Levi knows.

Group | Number of kinds
:- | -:
Beetles | $117$
Butterflies | $877$
Moths | $443$

**How many more kinds of butterflies than moths does Levi know?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kinds
hints:
Levi knows $\purpleD{877}$ kinds of butterflies. That equals the $\maroonD{443}$ kinds of moths Levi knows, plus some more kinds.

![A tape diagram models that the 877 kinds of butterflies are equal to the 443 kinds of moths and an unknown number of more kinds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bbd1c26854b9fe3a6ade860a1d52532d5b86460e)

$\purpleD{877}-\maroonD{443}=\blueE{?}$

![Part 1 of 2. Place value blocks show 877 blocks with 443 blocks crossed out](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c9a222f26e52e4a6bcce4c0666f21a7eec50e747)

![Part 2 of 2 of previous image.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4be66d57cfb491f463da8aed219f23469497ab92)
Levi knows $\blueE{434}$ more kinds of butterflies than moths.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table shows how many steps it takes each child to cross the playground.

Child | Steps
:- | -:
Anna | $675$
Jaiden | $783$
Maia | $567$
Cris | $583$

**How many fewer steps does Maia take than Cris?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] steps
hints:
Cris takes $\purpleD{583}$ steps. That equals the $\maroonD{567}$ steps Maia takes, plus some more steps.

![A tape diagram models that the 583 steps Cris takes are equal to the 567 steps Maia takes and an unknown number of more steps.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1277677e953271b808f9fb961189910aa527835d)

$\purpleD{583}-\maroonD{567}=\blueE{?}$

![Part 1 of 2. Place value blocks show 583 blocks with 567 blocks crossed out](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f160fcd031c789d292b060c5dfd9c4618edf073)

![Part 2 of 2 of previous image.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/58ac216c267204ba7d6294240416b5221d52a2e2)
Maia takes $\blueE{16}$ fewer steps than Cris.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table gives the amounts of materials Aya  recycled.

Material | Kilograms
:- | -:
Aluminum | $134$
Plastic | $85$
Glass | $57$
Paper | $277$

**How many kilograms of paper and aluminum did Aya recycle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilograms
hints:
We need to know the total number of kilograms of aluminum and paper.

![A tape diagram models that the 134 kilograms of aluminum and 277 kilograms of paper are equal to an unknown total number of kilograms.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb55bebef09a7db8e4ea8fad4575160f25d53f57)

$\maroonD{134}+\blueE{277} = \purpleD{?}$

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{134}+\blueE{277} &=\maroonD{100}+\blueE{200} + \maroonD{30} + \blueE{70} + \maroonD{4} + \blueE{7} \\\\
&= 300 + 100 +11
\\\\
&=\purpleD{411}
\end{align}$

![Place value blocks show 1 hundred plus 2 hundreds plus 3 tens plus 7 tens plus 4 plus 7](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/697c1b6cac8b7d5073b3c221c564edbbea41acb4)
Aya recycled $\purpleD{411}$ kilograms of aluminum and paper.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table shows how many times each child hopped in a relay.

Child | Hops
:- | -:
Brady | $73$
Gage | $62$
Jenny | $92$

**How many times did Brady and Jenny hop in all?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times
hints:
We need to know the total number of times Brady and Jenny hopped.

![A tape diagram models that Brady's 72 hops and Jenny's 92 hops are equal to an unknown total number of hops.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ca62e2ba2744b36cea610369dfcb4090718a14be)

$\maroonD{73}+\blueE{92} = \purpleD{?}$

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{73}+\blueE{92} &=\maroonD{70}+\blueE{90} + \maroonD{3} + \blueE{2} \\\\
&= 160 +5
\\\\
&=\purpleD{165}
\end{align}$

![Place value blocks show 7 tens plus 9 tens plus 3 plus 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/87b634d1cf84d8cf60d26820a9568082d5d73229)
Brady and Jenny hopped $\purpleD{165}$ times in all.

assessment_items.question_content:
A queen bee laid $514$ eggs on Saturday and $390$ eggs on Sunday.

**How many eggs did the bee lay?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] eggs
hints:
We need to know the total number of eggs on Saturday and Sunday.

![A tape diagram models that the 514 eggs on Saturday and 390 eggs on Sunday are equal to an unknown total number of eggs.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ae7cfd2c12d8b6d87e1b5c40b9559b71e0184211)

$\maroonD{514}+\blueE{390} = \purpleD{?}$

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{514}+\blueE{390} &=\maroonD{500}+\blueE{300} + \maroonD{10} + \blueE{90} + \maroonD{4} + \blueE{0} \\\\
&= 800 + 100 +4
\\\\
&=\purpleD{904}
\end{align}$

![Part 1 of 2. Place value blocks show 5 hundreds plus 3 hundreds plus 1 ten plus 9 tens plus 4 plus 0](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6380675a32530ff8e29e87e613023c015e524a07)

![Part 2 of 2 of previous image.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5cca1c32f95b1900ad3e9f917b4aa703fba00a84)
The bee laid $\purpleD{904}$ eggs in all.

assessment_items.question_content:
Aria played two strings on her guitar. The first string waved $330$ times and the second string waved $257$ more times than the first string.

**How many times did the second string wave?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times
hints:
We need to know the number of times the second string waved.

![A tape diagram models that the 330 times the first string waved and 257 more times are equal to an unknown number of times the second string waved.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3ff883db62944900bb5cd1284b6834926ca1a90b)

$\maroonD{330}+\blueE{257} = \purpleD{?}$

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{330}+\blueE{257} &=\maroonD{300}+\blueE{200} + \maroonD{30} + \blueE{50} + \maroonD{0} + \blueE{7} \\\\
&= 500 + 80 +7
\\\\
&=\purpleD{587}
\end{align}$

![Part 1 of 2. Place value blocks show 3 hundreds plus 2 hundreds plus 3 tens plus 5 tens plus 0 plus 7](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b4744bf91899cd15ce045ec9b4bd02b8459b7d12)

![Part 2 of 2 of previous image.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e9c58d14bf2fd7f8efb3459dcb227c8ca736ca4e)
The second string waved $\purpleD{587}$ times.

assessment_items.question_content:
So far, $57$ sea turtle eggs have hatched and $53$ eggs are still in the nest.

**How many eggs were there before any hatched?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] eggs
hints:
We need to know the total number of eggs before any hatched.

![A tape diagram models that the 57 hatched eggs and 53 eggs in the nest are equal to an unknown total number of eggs.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f6f4ad3ece4f3e638d075db9333da6741f5a75ce)

$\maroonD{57}+\blueE{53} = \purpleD{?}$

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{57}+\blueE{53} &=\maroonD{50}+\blueE{50} + \maroonD{7} + \blueE{3} \\\\
&= 100 +10
\\\\
&=\purpleD{110}
\end{align}$

![Place value blocks show 5 tens plus 5 tens plus 7 plus 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/42fcf217734d062c503b0d4bb1e41b90240ce9bc)
There were $\purpleD{110}$ eggs before any hatched.

assessment_items.question_content:
Frida used $168$ beads to make a necklace. Then she made another necklace with the same number of beads.

**How many beads did Frida use in all?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] beads
hints:
We need to know the total number of beads in the two necklaces.

![A tape diagram models that the 168 beads in the first necklace and 168 beads in the second necklace are equal to an unknown total number of beads.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a49d82dc6b335f9d4d690a1f510601ada1d1f431)

$\maroonD{168}+\blueE{168} = \purpleD{?}$

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{168}+\blueE{168} &=\maroonD{100}+\blueE{100} + \maroonD{60} + \blueE{60} + \maroonD{8} + \blueE{8} \\\\
&= 200 + 120 +16
\\\\
&=\purpleD{336}
\end{align}$

![Place value blocks show 1 hundred plus 1 hundred plus 6 tens plus 6 tens plus 8 plus 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ac66d6c8e262ec0cee2b5b12caa094c7799841fe)
Frida used $\purpleD{336}$ beads in all.
